theme: Stock stations before bikes run out
title: Coupled Bike-Share Restocking: Beat the Stockout Gradient, Not the Net Demand
strategies: equal split: divide the whole budget evenly across every station, clipped to dock capacity (baseline)

net-demand water-fill: rank stations by trips-originated-minus-trips-received over the whole horizon and fill the neediest first (the obvious recipe -- blind to WHEN each station's flow happens, and never touches the simulator)

sequential marginal-gradient construction: place bikes one at a time, each time replaying the schedule against the CURRENT allocation to find which station's next bike has the biggest real marginal payoff -- the coupled-flow stockout-probability gradient, queried directly rather than approximated
the marginal-gradient allocation polished by a short local search of single-bike swaps against the real joint simulator
